title: Habenaria muricata
uses: Habenaria muricata is known for its various medicinal uses. It is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate swelling and pain. Additionally, it is thought to possess antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in treating infections. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
taxonomy: Habenaria muricata is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Habenaria, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. The species is further classified under the tribe Orchideae. The exact classification within the Orchideae tribe is still being researched, but it is commonly accepted to be part of the subtribe Habenariinae. As of now, this classification is the most accurate information available for Habenaria muricata.

intro: Habenaria muricata, commonly known as the rough-leaved orchid, is a species of orchid found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as fever, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria muricata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Habenaria muricata is commonly prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
